message:
test(codex): add P2.2 deny + P2.3 allow leaf tests on the scaffold

Builds on the P2.1 scaffold (c0915bc) with the next two beads' contract
tests. Both rely on `run_codex_hook` and the `HookOutcome` postmortem
helpers; no scaffold changes.

P2.2 — git_safety_guard-ovw4.3.2 — Codex deny path:

- `codex_deny_multiple_destructive_commands` — parametrized over four
  destructives (`git reset --hard HEAD~5`, `git clean -fd`,
  `git push --force origin main`, `rm -rf /important/data`). For each:
  exit_code == 2, stdout 0 bytes (Codex's parser at codex-rs/hooks/src/
  events/pre_tool_use.rs:165 trims and treats empty as silent-allow,
  so we strictly require zero), stderr non-empty, stderr contains the
  expected rule-id fragment so the model gets the allowlist suggestion.
- `codex_deny_stderr_is_not_empty_even_when_nosuggest` — sanity guard
  for the catastrophic "exit 2 + empty stderr" path that codex parses
  as Failed (silently allows). Asserts stderr.len() > 10 to catch a
  print_colorful_warning regression where dcg flushed exit 2 but
  failed to write the deny block.

P2.3 — git_safety_guard-ovw4.3.3 — Codex allow path:

- `codex_allow_safe_commands_produce_no_output` — parametrized over
  seven near-destructive-looking-but-safe inputs (`git status`,
  `git log --oneline -5`, `git diff HEAD`, `git checkout -b
  new-feature`, `ls -la`, `echo hello`, `cat README.md`). Each must
  produce exit 0 + empty stdout. Negative-space coverage that catches
  a future change accidentally promoting a safe pattern to destructive.
- `codex_allow_git_clean_dry_run_not_blocked` — `git clean -n` must be
  allowed (dry-run override on a normally-destructive verb).

No scaffold changes; the new tests slot in between the existing smoke
tests and the hermetic-HOME assertion. Inline divider comments mark
the P2.2 / P2.3 / hermetic sections so future leaf tests have an
obvious place to land.
